content.text: Let me test if the color is actually being applied at all in the real CSS, by using an obvious color:

input.command:
pkill waybar
# Temporarily change label color to bright green to confirm the rule is firing
sed 's/label {/label { /' /home/sn0w/.dotfiles/.config/waybar/style.css | grep -A2 "^label"
input.description: Check current label rule in CSS

input.command:
cp /home/sn0w/.dotfiles/.config/waybar/style.css /tmp/waybar-backup.css
sed -i 's/label {/label-TEMP {/' /home/sn0w/.dotfiles/.config/waybar/style.css
# Add green label rule at end
echo -e "\nlabel { color: #00ff00; }" >> /home/sn0w/.dotfiles/.config/waybar/style.css
GTK_THEME=Adwaita:dark waybar &>/tmp/waybar.log &
sleep 1.5
echo "running - check if text is green"
input.description: Test with bright green to confirm label selector fires

content.content: running - check if text is green

content.text: So right-side modules aren't `label` widgets — they must be `button` containing a label, or `eventbox`. Let me test:

input.command:
pkill waybar
# Test: add button and button > label targeting
cat >> /home/sn0w/.dotfiles/.config/waybar/style.css << 'EOF'
button { color: #00ff00; }
button label { color: #00ff00; }
EOF
GTK_THEME=Adwaita:dark waybar &>/tmp/waybar.log &
sleep 1.5
echo "check if right side is green now"
input.description: Test button selector for right-side modules

content.content: check if right side is green now

toolUseResult.stdout: check if right side is green now

content.text: Is the right-side text (clock, IP, etc.) green now?

message.content: no

input.command:
pkill waybar
# Try every possible GTK3 container node type
cat >> /home/sn0w/.dotfiles/.config/waybar/style.css << 'EOF'
eventbox label { color: #00ff00; }
box > label { color: #00ff00; }
EOF
GTK_THEME=Adwaita:dark waybar &>/tmp/waybar.log &
sleep 1.5
echo "check"
input.description: Test eventbox and box label selectors

content.content: check

content.text: Is any of the right side green?

message.content: no

file.content:
general {
    gaps_in = 5
    gaps_out = 10
    border_size = 2
    col.active_border = rgb(ffffff)

input.old_string:
layerrule = blur on, match:namespace waybar
layerrule = blur on, match:namespace launcher
layerrule = blur on, match:namespace wofi
input.new_string:
layerrule = blur on, match:namespace waybar
layerrule = blur on, match:namespace launcher
layerrule = blur on, match:namespace wofi
layerrule = blur on, match:namespace swaync-control-center
layerrule = ignorezero on, match:namespace swaync-control-center

content.content: The file /home/sn0w/.dotfiles/.config/hypr/hyprland/general.conf has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

toolUseResult.oldString:
layerrule = blur on, match:namespace waybar
layerrule = blur on, match:namespace launcher
layerrule = blur on, match:namespace wofi
toolUseResult.newString:
layerrule = blur on, match:namespace waybar
layerrule = blur on, match:namespace launcher
layerrule = blur on, match:namespace wofi
layerrule = blur on, match:namespace swaync-control-center
layerrule = ignorezero on, match:namespace swaync-control-center
toolUseResult.originalFile:
general {
    gaps_in = 5
    gaps_out = 10
    border_size = 2
    col.active_border = rgb(ffffff)
    col.inactive_border = rgba(808080b3) rgba(ffffffb3) 45deg
    resize_on_border = false
    allow_tearing = false
    layout = dwindle
}

decoration {
    rounding = 10
    rounding_power = 2
    active_opacity = 0.97
    inactive_opacity = 0.90

    shadow {
        enabled = true
        range = 4
        render_power = 3
        color = rgba(1a1a1aee)
    }

    blur {
        enabled = true
        size = 5
        passes = 2
        vibrancy = 0.17
    }
}

layerrule = blur on, match:namespace waybar
layerrule = blur on, match:namespace launcher
layerrule = blur on, match:namespace wofi

animations {
    enabled = yes
    bezier = easeOutQuint,0.23,1,0.32,1
    bezier = easeInOutCubic,0.65,0.05,0.36,1
    bezier = linear,0,0,1,1
    bezier = almostLinear,0.5,0.5,0.75,1.0
    bezier = quick,0.15,0,0.1,1

    animation = global, 1, 10, default
    animation = border, 1, 5.39, easeOutQuint
    animation = windows, 1, 4.79, easeOutQuint
    animation = windowsIn, 1, 4.1, easeOutQuint, popin 87%
    animation = windowsOut, 1, 1.49, linear, popin 87%
    animation = fadeIn, 1, 1.73, almostLinear
    animation = fadeOut, 1, 1.46, almostLinear
    animation = fade, 1, 3.03, quick
    animation = layers, 1, 3.81, easeOutQuint
    animation = layersIn, 1, 4, easeOutQuint, fade
    animation = layersOut, 1, 1.5, linear, fade
    animation = fadeLayersIn, 1, 1.79, almostLinear
    animation = fadeLayersOut, 1, 1.39, almostLinear
    animation = workspaces, 1, 1.94, almostLinear, fade
    animation = workspacesIn, 1, 1.21, almostLinear, fade
    animation = workspacesOut, 1, 1.94, almostLinear, fade
}

dwindle {
    pseudotile = true
    preserve_split = true
}

master {
    new_status = master
}

misc {
    force_default_wallpaper = 0
    disable_hyprland_logo = true
    disable_autoreload = true
}

windowrule {
    name = looking-glass-opacity
    match:class = looking-glass-client
    opacity = 1 override
}

windowrule {
    name = zen-opacity
    match:class = zen
    opacity = 1 override
}

# Disable transparency for fullscreen windows (games, video, etc.)
windowrule {
    name = fullscreen-opacity
    match:fullscreen = true
    opacity = 1 override
}

# Allow WoW to be centered, fullscreen, and fix shaking
windowrule {
    name = wow-rules
    match:class = steam_app_default
    match:title = ^(World of Warcraft)$
    float = yes
    fullscreen = yes
    immediate = yes
}

content.text: The swaync layer covers the full screen but only draws the sidebar in part of it — so blur gets applied to everything behind the transparent area. The fix is `ignorealpha` which skips blurring transparent pixels:

input.new_string:
layerrule = blur on, match:namespace swaync-control-center
layerrule = ignorealpha 0.1, match:namespace swaync-control-center

content.text: The problem is swaync creates a fullscreen transparent layer — blur applies to all of it, not just the sidebar. Since `ignorealpha` is invalid in this Hyprland version, the cleanest fix is to remove the blur layerrule and compensate with a more opaque background in the CSS:

content.text: Now make the swaync sidebar background more opaque so it still looks good without blur:

content.content: The file /home/sn0w/.dotfiles/.config/swaync/style.css has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
The file /home/sn0w/.dotfiles/.config/swaync/style.css has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

input.command: hyprctl reload 2>&1 && swaync-client -R && swaync-client -rs 2>/dev/null

content.content:
ok
CSS reload success: true

content.text: No more fullscreen blur. The sidebar now has an 85% opaque dark background matching the theme — solid enough to read without bleeding blur across the whole screen.

message.content: so its not possible to only blur this one panel on the right?

content.text:
Not really, no — it's a fundamental limitation of how swaync works. It creates a **fullscreen layer surface** covering the entire screen, but only draws the sidebar panel on the right portion of it. Hyprland's blur applies to the whole layer surface, so the transparent areas blur everything behind them too.

The only way around it would be if swaync created a layer surface that was sized exactly to the sidebar (not fullscreen), which it doesn't support.

Some workarounds people use:
- **What we have now** — opaque dark background, no layerrule blur
- **Fake blur** — use a very dark semi-transparent background (`rgba(18,19,24,0.92)`) so it looks like frosted glass without actual blur
- **Switch to a different notification daemon** like `mako` or `dunst`, though those don't have a sidebar UI

Want to tweak the opacity/color of the background to get it looking closer to what you had in mind?

message.content: so youd take a screenshot of the portion and blur and darken it?

content.text:
That's exactly the idea — a "fake blur" script that:
1. Screenshots the panel area before swaync opens
2. Blurs + darkens it with imagemagick
3. Uses it as the CSS background image

Let me check if the tools are available first:

input.command: which grim convert magick 2>/dev/null; cat /etc/xdg/swaync/config.json | grep -i "width\|position\|margin" | head -10
input.description: Check grim and imagemagick availability, swaync panel width

content.content:
/usr/bin/grim
/usr/bin/convert
/usr/bin/magick
  "positionX": "right",
  "positionY": "top",
  "control-center-margin-top": 0,
  "control-center-margin-bottom": 0,
  "control-center-margin-right": 0,
  "control-center-margin-left": 0,
  "notification-body-image-width": 200,
  "control-center-width": 500,
  "notification-window-width": 500,

content.text: You're out of extra usage · resets 10pm (Europe/Vienna)

lastPrompt: so youd take a screenshot of the portion and blur and darken it?
